name: Vushka
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour and salt, blending well.
2. Add the milk, egg yolk, and oil, again blending well. Allow to rest for 5 minutes.
3. Knead for about 10 minutes until a smooth ball forms (this can be done in a food processor).
4. Cover and set aside for 15 minutes or so.
5. On a floured surface, roll out one-third of the dough into a rectangle that is 1/8 inch thick.
6. Turn the dough from the center to ensure even thickness throughout the rectangle.
7. Run your hand under the dough to loosen it, then dust with flour and flip over.
8. Dust again with flour, then cut into 1 1/2-inch squares using a sharp knife.
9. Place a teaspoon of mushroom filling (see the next recipe in this series) in each square, being careful not to smear the edges.
10. Fold on the diagonal to make a triangle and pinch together the two bottom corners, wrapping it around your thumb with the point up to resemble small ears on a cat.
11. Ensure the edges are securely bonded; otherwise, the stuffing may bubble out.
12. Place the vushka on cookie sheets covered with dish towels lightly dusted with flour.
13. Repeat with the remaining dough, saving scraps for last as the dough becomes tougher when worked.
14. Drop 10-12 vushka into 6-8 cups of rapidly boiling water and stir once with a wooden spoon (a metal spoon will lower the water temperature).
15. Do not cover; when they float to the top, cook for 1 minute, then remove with a slotted spoon to a strainer.
16. Cool on a lightly oiled plate without crowding.
17. Repeat until all are cooked. Cover and set aside.
18. These can be frozen and reheated in boiling water (do not overcook when reheating).
To Serve:
Place 4-5 vushka in soup plates and pour hot borsch over them.
